name: Japan Open
stakes: This Super 750 event in Tokyo offers India's shuttlers a vital opportunity to consolidate recent gains, find crucial consistency, and make a significant statement on the BWF World Tour.
summary:
India arrives in Tokyo for the Japan Open, a Super 750 event, with a mixed bag of recent performances but a clear hunger for deep runs. The undeniable highlight comes from the men's doubles duo, **Satwiksairaj Rankireddy & Chirag Shetty**, who are fresh off a spectacular Singapore Open title, where they defeated the reigning world champions. Their form suggests they are strong contenders to go all the way here.

In men's singles, **Lakshya Sen** and **HS Prannoy** will be looking for redemption after early exits at the Singapore Open, with Sen even copping a yellow card in his loss. Meanwhile, **Kidambi Srikanth** showed promising form by reaching the US Open final, and young **Rounak Chouhan** made waves by taking down the World No. 6 at the same event—signs of growing depth in the singles ranks. **PV Sindhu** continues her journey back to peak form, having made the second round at the Indonesia Open, and will be eager to push further.

The women's doubles pair of **Treesa Jolly & Gayatri Gopichand** remain a key focus as they build towards the Asian Games, while the mixed doubles pair **Tanisha Crasto & Dhruv Kapila** have shown flashes of brilliance, including a win over a top Japanese pair in Singapore. With the draw yet to be revealed, anticipation builds for who will face tough openers and who might find a clearer path to the later stages in this crucial tournament.

indiaContingent.note: Lost at Singapore Open with a yellow card

indiaContingent.name: HS Prannoy
indiaContingent.note: Lost to Loh Kean Yew at Singapore Open

indiaContingent.note: US Open finalist, showing strong form

indiaContingent.name: Rounak Chouhan
indiaContingent.note: US Open giant-killer, beating World No. 6

indiaContingent.note: Beat top Japanese pair at Singapore Open
